behaviors.description: Detect stale branches when commits are ahead of main, older than threshold, and have no open PR.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is at module handoff (git-state computation + PR-state lookup + threshold policy), not a single pure function. REAL-INFRA: behavior text does not require live repo/API, so System is not mandatory. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: no model output dependency or agent pipeline. Rejection gate: rejected System because the behavior does not require 'live repo' or real HTTP/subprocess semantics; it is about combining computed states.
behaviors.test_description: Wire branch-state collector output, PR-state provider output, and staleness evaluator in one test with in-process fixtures: ahead-of-main=true, oldest commit age>threshold, open_pr=false => stale=true; vary one predicate at a time to ensure conjunction logic catches real cross-module mapping failures.

behaviors.description: Session bootstrap emits stale-work warning with actionable branch context when current branch is stale.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: bug appears when startup wiring, stale-state input, and notifier/renderer interact. REAL-INFRA: no requirement for real OS/process/network; can run in-process bootstrap harness. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection gate: rejected System because behavior is emission/wiring at session start, not real subprocess/HTTP behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Run session-start initialization with injected stale branch state and capture emitted warning channel; assert warning is emitted once and includes branch name, age, and next action text. This catches missing wiring/context propagation that unit tests of formatter or detector alone would miss.

behaviors.description: Local classifier deterministically maps computed branch state to labels `clean` and `stale-no-pr`.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: behavior is explicitly one local function mapping input state to label. No module boundary is required to expose the failure. REAL-INFRA: none. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection gate: rejected Integration+ because behavior text says 'local status-classifier function' and deterministic mapping.
behaviors.test_description: Table-driven pure function test over branch-state tuples (ahead, age, pr_status) asserting exact label output and no nondeterminism across repeated calls.

behaviors.description: `/kaizen-wip` using live repo and live PR state renders `clean` vs `STALE` correctly.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: behavior explicitly says 'against live repo and PR state', requiring real git/remote state and likely real subprocess/HTTP interactions. MOCK-HIDE: stubs can mask integration failures in command execution, auth, pagination, or state freshness. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: no model dependency. Rejection gate: rejected Integration because behavior text requires live infrastructure semantics.
behaviors.test_description: Execute `/kaizen-wip` end-to-end in a real test repository with actual branch divergence and real PR state (open/none), then assert rendered output shows `clean` or `has commits but no PR (STALE)` correctly.

behaviors.description: Open or merged PR branches are not false-positively labeled `stale-no-pr`.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is usually at boundary between PR-state interpretation and stale classifier (e.g., treating merged/open as none). REAL-INFRA: behavior does not require live API; representative PR-state fixtures can expose logic/regression. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection gate: rejected System because no explicit live/network requirement in the behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Integrate PR-state adapter output with classifier: cases for `open`, `merged`, `closed-unmerged`, and `none`; assert only `none` can produce `stale-no-pr` when other stale predicates are true. This catches cross-module enum/field mapping bugs.

behaviors.description: Detection stays correct after rebase/squash merge history rewrites where commit hashes change.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: rebase/squash correctness depends on real git graph rewrite behavior and ancestry resolution, typically via git subprocess + filesystem repo state. MOCK-HIDE: mocked commit lists often miss edge cases in rewritten DAG semantics. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection gate: rejected Integration because behavior specifically targets rebase/squash hash-change scenarios that are semantic git-history effects best reproduced with real git operations.
behaviors.test_description: In a real local git repo, create stale candidate branch, then perform rebase and squash-merge scenarios that rewrite hashes; rerun staleness detection and assert branch is no longer flagged as unPRed stale when effective work is already integrated.
